refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve an operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was mostly atomic, focusing on meeting or exceeding a customer-defined availability target. It was refined to explicitly state the measurement context (operational availability) and the evaluation period, ensuring clarity and solution neutrality without implying any implementation details.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the operational availability of the NGHE solution over the agreed evaluation period and compare it against the customer-defined target to verify compliance.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall achieve an operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.

candidate_dps: Design system components and processes to minimize downtime and enable rapid recovery
Implement continuous monitoring and diagnostics to detect and address faults proactively
Incorporate redundancy and failover mechanisms to maintain operation during component failures
Establish maintenance and operational procedures aligned with safety and policy requirements to support availability
selected_dp.selected_dp: Incorporate redundancy and failover mechanisms to maintain operation during component failures

potential_sources: Redundancy mechanisms may interact with monitoring and maintenance processes
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on logical independence by enabling failover without requiring tight coupling between components. Monitoring and maintenance DPs can be modular and interface through well-defined protocols.

assumptions_risks: Availability target is achievable given system complexity and operational environment
Operational policies and safety plans are compatible with redundancy and failover strategies
Measurement methods for availability are accurate and consistent
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including monitoring and maintenance DPs as complementary to redundancy to ensure comprehensive availability management.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for ensuring system safety, including redundancy and failover mechanisms to maintain operation during component failures

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts when failover or redundancy mechanisms are triggered

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives system status and health data to monitor component failures and redundancy status

module_containment_scores.notes: Very broad module; contains DP but with high diffusion as redundancy spans multiple subsystems.
Contains physical components but redundancy mechanisms may involve software and external services, causing moderate diffusion.
Focused on remote control; redundancy mechanisms are peripheral here, leading to poor containment and high diffusion.
Specialized in safety-related functions including redundancy and failover; best containment and minimal diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Safety Services module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the DP of incorporating redundancy and failover mechanisms, as it is dedicated to safety functions and can centralize these mechanisms effectively with minimal diffusion outside the module.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on telemetry data from other modules may create coupling.
If redundancy mechanisms require coordination with Vehicle or Teleoperation modules, interface complexity may increase.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of incorporating redundancy and failover mechanisms directly addresses the FR of achieving operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target by maintaining operation during component failures, which aligns well with the acceptance criteria of measured availability ≥ 0.97.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on telemetry data from other modules may create coupling.
Coordination with Vehicle or Teleoperation modules may increase interface complexity and diffusion risk.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Safety Services module shows the highest containment score (9) and lowest diffusion score (2), indicating it is the most appropriate and focused boundary to contain the DP. This minimizes diffusion and centralizes redundancy and failover mechanisms effectively.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring the Safety Services module outputs such as SafetyAlert and Telemetry interfaces, which provide data on failover triggers and system health relevant to availability metrics.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the Safety Services module is clear and well-justified, with explicit scope and interfaces defined to support the FR.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define and manage interfaces between Safety Services and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules to minimize coupling and interface complexity.
Document coordination protocols for redundancy mechanisms that span multiple modules to reduce diffusion risk.
Consider monitoring and logging strategies within Safety Services to support verification of availability targets.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear alignment, containment, and verification feasibility. The DP of incorporating redundancy and failover mechanisms sufficiently addresses the FR of achieving the required operational availability, and the Safety Services module is an appropriate implementation boundary with manageable diffusion risks. Given the high safety criticality, the current level is adequately detailed to proceed with implementation and verification without further decomposition at this stage.

revise.why: To explicitly define and manage interfaces between Safety Services and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules to minimize coupling and interface complexity, and to document coordination protocols and monitoring strategies supporting verification.

assumptions: The DP fully satisfies the FR acceptance criteria for availability ≥ 0.97.
The Safety Services module can effectively contain the redundancy and failover mechanisms with minimal diffusion risk.
open_risks: Potential dependency on telemetry data from other modules may create coupling.
Coordination with Vehicle or Teleoperation modules may increase interface complexity and diffusion risk.
evidence_needed: Verification data from Safety Services module outputs such as SafetyAlert and Telemetry interfaces demonstrating failover triggers and system health.
Documentation of interface definitions and coordination protocols between Safety Services and other modules.
